messages.content: I want you to become my Prompt Creator. Your goal is to help me craft the best possible prompt for my needs. The prompt will be used by you, ChatGPT. You will follow the following process: 1. Your first response will be to ask me what the prompt should be about. I will provide my answer, but we will need to improve it through continual iterations by going through the next steps. 2. Based on my input, you will generate 3 sections. a) Revised prompt (provide your rewritten prompt. it should be clear, concise, and easily understood by you), b) Suggestions (provide suggestions on what details to include in the prompt to improve it), and c) Questions (ask any relevant questions pertaining to what additional information is needed from me to improve the prompt). 3. We will continue this iterative process with me providing additional information to you and you updating the prompt in the Revised prompt section until it's complete.
Sure, I'd be happy to help you create the best possible prompt! What would you like the prompt to be about?
A generalized format for writing stylization and vocabulary in the form of a matrix using brackets and commas

Thank you for providing the prompt topic. Here are the three sections for prompt creation:

Revised prompt: Develop a matrix-based system that provides a generalized format for writing style and vocabulary, using brackets and commas.

Suggestions:
- Specify the purpose and context of the writing that the stylization and vocabulary are being developed for.
- Outline various elements that would need to be included in the matrix (i.e. parts of speech, tone, register, etc.).
- Clarify how the brackets and commas will be utilized in the matrix system.

Questions:
- What type of written content is this matrix being created for (i.e. academic papers, creative writing, business documents, etc.)?
- What specific aspects of style and vocabulary do you want the matrix to cover?
- How do you envision the brackets and commas being used in this matrix system?
(1. Stories) (2. Writing style, tag types, detail amount, having dialogue (both internal and external), special words to use, etc) (3. Each bracket is a different elements and is labeled.)

Thank you for your response. Here is the updated prompt:

Revised prompt: Develop a matrix-based system for writing short stories that includes selected writing style, tag types, internal and external dialogue, detail amount, and special words. The system will use brackets labeled with different elements.

Suggestions:
- Further specify the intended audience or genre for the short stories (e.g. young adult fiction, horror, romance, etc.).
- Consider the length of the short stories that the system will be used for and how the matrix system can be tailored to fit those constraints.
- Provide examples of the types of labels and elements that will be included in the matrix system.

Questions:
- Are there specific writing style and tag types that you want included in the matrix system?
- How will the system use the brackets to differentiate between the different elements?
- Are there any specific words or phrases that you want to be incorporated into the matrix system?
(1. Have it be used specified in a user specification section) (2. A format such as [type: data], [type2: data]

Thank you for your response. Here is the updated prompt:

Revised prompt: Develop a matrix-based system that can be customized based on a user specification section, which should include options for writing style, tag types, internal and external dialogue, detail amount, and special words. The matrix system will use a format that includes brackets for labeling elements, such as [type: data] and [type2: data].

Suggestions:
- Provide clear and concise instructions for how users can customize the matrix system based on the provided specification section.
- Determine how the system will handle options that conflict with each other.
- Consider implementing a validation system to ensure that the user specifications are valid and properly formatted.

Questions:
- Can you provide an example of how a user specification section might look?
- How will the matrix system handle cases where the user does not specify certain options?
- Are there any specific tags or elements that you want to include in the matrix system, aside from those previously mentioned?
(1. Style: newline tags: newline etc) (2. It reads inputted data and generates other data based on the given information) (3. No)

Thank you for your response. Here is the updated prompt:

Revised prompt: Develop a matrix-based system that can be customized based on a user specification section that includes options for writing style and tag types such as newline tags. The system should read inputted data and generate other data based on the provided information.

Suggestions:
- Determine the type of data that the system will generate based on the input.
- Provide clear instructions for how the system will read the input.
- Consider implementing a preview feature that allows the user to see how their input will be formatted before generating the final content.

Questions:
- Can you provide more information about what kind of data the system will generate?
- Are there any specific requirements for how the input data is structured or formatted?
- How detailed should the instructions be for using the user specification section in the matrix system?
(1. This was detailed above see previous postings) (2. No) (3. Detailed enough, but short)

Thank you for your response. Here is the final prompt:

Develop a matrix-based system that can be customized based on a user specification section that includes options for writing style and tag types such as newline tags. The system should read inputted data and generate other data based on the provided information.

The matrix system will use a format that includes brackets for labeling elements, such as [type: data] and [type2: data]. The user specification section should be sufficiently detailed but concise. Consider implementing a preview feature that allows the user to see how their input will be formatted before generating the final content.

No further questions.

Develop a matrix-based system that can be customized based on a user specification section that includes options for writing style and tag types such as newline tags. The system should read inputted data and generate other data based on the provided information.

The matrix system will use a format that includes brackets for labeling elements, such as [type: data] and [type2: data]. The user specification section should be sufficiently detailed but concise. Consider implementing a preview feature that allows the user to see how their input will be formatted before generating the final content.
Great, glad we were able to work together to create a clear and concise prompt that meets your needs! Let me know if you need any further assistance.

Develop a matrix-based system that can be customized based on a user specification section that includes options for writing style and tag types such as newline tags. The system should read inputted data and generate other data based on the provided information.

The matrix system will use a format that includes brackets for labeling elements, such as [type: data] and [type2: data]. The user specification section should be sufficiently detailed but concise. Consider implementing a preview feature that allows the user to see how their input will be formatted before generating the final content.
Yes, that is the final version of the prompt we developed together. Let me know if you need any more assistance.
